response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong web and full-stack engineer with a consistent TypeScript/Node.js trajectory, but this role demands deep, sustained C++ expertise and industrial automation domain knowledge he does not demonstrate. His only formal C++ work was a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project; C++ does not even appear in his current skills list. There is no evidence of multi-threaded networking systems, industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), IEC 62443 or SDL practices, or IoT platform development — all of which are core requirements for this position.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven and distributed-systems thinking through the GISCollective platform built on Node.js and Kubernetes
Consistent application of agile engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple roles
Broad multi-language exposure including a brief C++ background, showing adaptability across technology stacks
concerns: C++ experience is negligible and over a decade old — not listed as a current skill and insufficient for a senior C++ engineering role
Zero exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, SDL practices, or relevant protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
No evidence of formal team leadership, architecture ownership at scale, or mentoring responsibilities expected at the Senior Manager level
